codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 3 BLOCKERs (CAS underspecification, ABA risk, unsafe Option-B fallback), 9 MAJORs (schema stream discriminator missing, reason field overloading, multi-agent batch atomicity gap, lock scope unclear, reader consistency strategy missing, uncapped_delta naming ambiguity, cache source-of-truth vague, test impact understated, exit-code contract incomplete, JSONL growth/merge risk under-severity), 1 MINOR (caller count wording), and 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY. All BLOCKERs and MAJORs incorporated.

findings.issue: CAS is underspecified: expected_prev_score has no source; callers pass no expected score, so lock-then-read-then-append is serialized append, not real CAS
findings.proposed_fix: Add --expected-prev-score <n> and optionally --expected-prev-event-id <id> CLI parameters to score-update.sh; compare both under the lifecycle lock. If omitted, document as serialized append, not CAS.
findings.rationale: Core correctness issue with the proposed CAS mechanism. Incorporated into the CAS design analysis.

findings.issue: ABA risk: score-only CAS can pass after intervening events when score returns to same value
findings.proposed_fix: Add event_id (uuid4), seq (monotone int per agent), prev_event_id to JSONL schema. CAS must compare expected_prev_event_id or (agent, seq), not just score.
findings.rationale: Real correctness defect in proposed CAS design. Incorporated into schema recommendations.

findings.issue: Option B fallback is unsafe once JSONL is partially populated: per-agent check of empty JSONL fails after first event for any one agent while others are still absent
findings.proposed_fix: Option B-prime: append one score_baseline_import entry per canonical agent from current agent-scores.json (delta:0, prev_score:null, new_score:current, source:agent-scores.json) before accepting any new events.
findings.rationale: Real migration defect. Incorporated into migration plan.

findings.issue: Schema lacks lifecycle stream discriminator; score rows can collide with non-score rows from W3/W5 quota-wall and resumption events in same file
findings.proposed_fix: Add kind:"score_update" field to every score entry; readers filter by kind.
findings.rationale: Real schema gap given lifecycle.jsonl is shared with other event types per spec R9/W3/W5.

findings.issue: reason:"task-id" is overloaded; mixes human text, task ID, and correlation
findings.proposed_fix: Explicit fields: task_id, actor, source_command, note, correlation_id. Keep reason for human rationale only.
findings.rationale: Schema clarity issue with real query/audit impact.

findings.issue: Multi-agent close/rating updates are not transactionally modeled; CAS exit-3 on one agent leaves partial batch written
findings.proposed_fix: Add batch_id, batch_index, batch_size to schema; update caller docs to report partial success; or implement --batch JSON call under one lock.
findings.rationale: Real atomicity gap for the 3-agent close-event pattern.

findings.issue: Locking plan does not specify read-latest and append occur under the SAME lifecycle lock
findings.proposed_fix: Explicit protocol: open logs/lifecycle.jsonl.lock; exclusive flock; scan tail for agent; validate CAS; append one JSON line; fsync; release lock.
findings.rationale: Without this, the CAS protocol has a TOCTOU window.

findings.issue: Readers (score-inject.sh) have no consistency strategy against concurrent writer appends
findings.proposed_fix: Take shared flock on same lock file during reads, OR guarantee one-line atomic appends and make parser skip only a final unterminated line.
findings.rationale: Real torn-read risk on JSONL reads.

findings.issue: uncapped_delta name is ambiguous; current value is old_score + delta (unclamped target score), not a delta
findings.proposed_fix: Use unclamped_score in JSONL; preserve uncapped_delta as backward-compat alias. Note: pre-existing in agent-scores.json history, so this is an additive field in JSONL.
findings.rationale: Schema clarity. Naming is pre-existing in agent-scores.json but JSONL is a new schema, so correct naming should be established now.

findings.issue: Cache strategy too vague: 'rebuilt on-demand OR maintained as read-cache' leaves source-of-truth ambiguous
findings.proposed_fix: State explicitly: lifecycle.jsonl is the only source of truth. agent-scores.json is bootstrap-only until baseline import, then a derived cache rebuilt atomically under lifecycle lock with metadata {source_log_size, source_log_sha256}.
findings.rationale: Source-of-truth ambiguity is an architectural defect.

findings.issue: Test impact is understated; test-inject-branches.sh builds JSON fixtures not JSONL, and test_AC4 already fails (expects delta +8 but current emits +1)
findings.proposed_fix: Full test impact list: (1) tests/score-inject-contract/ needs JSONL fixtures; (2) test_AC4 needs delta assertion from current EVENT_DELTAS; (3) new tests needed: CAS conflict, baseline-import, malformed JSONL.
findings.rationale: Runtime-verified: codex ran test_AC4 and got assertion failure.

findings.issue: Exit-code contract incomplete: exit 3 added but malformed JSON currently exits 1 via Python traceback, not 2
findings.proposed_fix: Define and enforce: 0=success, 1=usage/validation/unknown-agent-or-event, 2=lock/IO/parse-error, 3=CAS-conflict. Update commands/dev.md:904 and close.md to capture and report exit 3 explicitly.
findings.rationale: Exit code clarity needed for callers to distinguish CAS conflict from parse failure.

findings.issue: Tracked JSONL growth/merge risk under-severity: lifecycle.jsonl in git means every score event dirties working tree, causing git conflicts across concurrent sessions
findings.proposed_fix: Explicit operational decision required: either accept tracked churn and document commit/rotation policy, or use tracked snapshots plus ignored runtime log (separate approved spec change).
findings.rationale: This is a structural architecture decision, not just a risk note.

findings.issue: Caller count wording inaccurate: dev.md calls score-inject 3x but score-update 1-2x; close.md calls score-update 3x for close plus optionally 3x for rating
findings.proposed_fix: Replace 'commands/dev.md calls score-update 2-3x per cycle from close event handling' with accurate description.
findings.rationale: Minor but affects downstream BA spec accuracy.

findings.issue: Option A migration wording: backwards chaining is unnecessary since history already has old_score/new_score; risks masking broken chains
findings.proposed_fix: If full migration chosen, transform each history item directly from old_score/new_score, validate chain continuity, emit migration report for gaps.
findings.rationale: OBSERVATION_ONLY per Codex. Logged for BA to consider if Option A is selected.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 3 BLOCKERs applied: CAS protocol redesigned with explicit --expected-prev-score parameter and event_id/seq/prev_event_id for ABA prevention; Option B replaced with Option B-prime baseline-import. All 9 MAJORs applied: schema updated with kind discriminator, structured fields, batch modeling, explicit lock protocol, reader flock strategy, unclamped_score naming, source-of-truth declaration, full test impact list, exit-code table, and JSONL-in-git operational decision requirement. 1 MINOR applied (caller count). OBSERVATION_ONLY logged.

app_understanding.notes: This is a CLI/shell-script architecture review; no web app or browser testing applies. Analysis is code-only with runtime file inspection.

runtime_artifacts_inspected: logs/lifecycle.jsonl (0 bytes, empty, tracked in git)
scripts/score-update.sh (7130 bytes, flock+Python heredoc, exit codes 0/1/2)
scripts/score-inject.sh (6897 bytes, reads agent-scores.json, emits INJECTION_PROOF block)
commands/dev.md:348,652,810,897-904 (3 score-inject calls + 1-2 score-update calls per cycle)
commands/close.md:415-459 (3 close score-update calls + optional 3 rating calls)
settings.json: Bash(scripts/score-update.sh:*) and Bash(~/.claude/scripts/score-update.sh:*) both allowlisted
hooks/doc_sync/tree.py:26 and regen_readme.py:31: agent-scores.json in SKIP_FILES

console_errors_checked.note: N/A for CLI; Codex ran scripts and verified exit codes at runtime

network_requests_analyzed.note: N/A for CLI; file I/O patterns analyzed instead

pages_visited.note: CLI tool review; browser pages not applicable

runtime_metrics_collected.note: File sizes, entry counts, and script exit codes measured

bundle_size_checked.note: N/A for shell scripts; script sizes measured

performance_benchmarked.note: File scan cost for JSONL tail-reads analyzed

runtime_findings.observation: 53973 bytes; 21 canonical agents; ba=70 history entries, dev=74, qa=57, all others=0; 35/70 ba entries have uncapped_delta field (entries before Cycle 2 patch lack it); latest ba entry: {ts:2026-05-25T05:03:00Z, event:close_fail_qa_pass, delta:-5, old_score:70, new_score:65, uncapped_delta:65}
runtime_findings.notes: History schema is heterogeneous: older entries lack uncapped_delta field. This must be accounted for in both migration plan and score-inject.sh fallback logic.

runtime_findings.observation: 0 bytes; empty file; tracked in git (gitignore exception confirmed); located at /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/logs/lifecycle.jsonl
runtime_findings.notes: File is ready to receive entries but has never been written to.

runtime_findings.observation: Flock on SCORES_FILE.lock (fd 9), 10s wait-timeout. Python heredoc does: json.load, mutate in memory, tempfile+os.replace (atomic). Exit 0=success, 1=invalid arg/unknown event (Python sys.exit(1)), 2=lock timeout. Malformed JSON currently propagates Python traceback to stderr and exits via the `set -euo pipefail` shell, producing exit 1 not exit 2.
runtime_findings.notes: Exit code for parse error is inconsistent with the documented intent; malformed JSONL error handling must be designed explicitly.

runtime_findings.observation: Reads SCORES_FILE (default ~/.claude/agent-scores.json); if file missing emits neutral mid-tier fallback (exits 0). If agent not in file, also emits neutral mid-tier (no error). Python parses full JSON, extracts latest 3 history events for the named agent, emits INJECTION_PROOF block with sha256[:8] digest.
runtime_findings.notes: score-inject.sh has no awareness of JSONL; full rewrite required to parse lifecycle.jsonl instead.

runtime_findings.observation: score-inject.sh called 3 times per cycle: --agent ba (step 1 before BA dispatch), --agent dev (step 6 before Dev dispatch), --agent qa (step 11 before QA dispatch). Commands/dev.md also calls score-update.sh 1-2x: 0 times if qa_first_pass (delta=0), or 1-2 times for reject events (qa_reject_dev, qa_reject_ba).
runtime_findings.notes: score-inject is the hot path (3 reads/cycle). JSONL scan cost is a real performance consideration.

runtime_findings.observation: score-update.sh called 3x for close outcome (dev+ba+qa) and optionally 3x for user rating. All 6 calls are sequential with no batch mechanism. Each call acquires and releases its own lock independently.
runtime_findings.notes: 6 sequential lock acquisitions per close cycle. Under CAS, each of these would independently read latest state and CAS-compare, making partial-batch writes possible if any call fails.

runtime_findings.observation: agent-scores.json and agent-scores.json.lock are in the SKIP_FILES exclusion list for doc-sync. This means if agent-scores.json is deprecated and lifecycle.jsonl becomes the primary, the SKIP_FILES list will need updating to include lifecycle.jsonl (or it will appear in doc-sync output).
runtime_findings.notes: Breaking change surface not listed in the draft: doc_sync SKIP_FILES requires update.

current_architecture_summary.write_path: score-update.sh acquires exclusive flock on agent-scores.json.lock (FD 9, 10s timeout), then Python heredoc reads the full JSON, mutates the in-memory data structure, and writes atomically via tempfile+os.replace. The flock prevents concurrent writers but does not prevent TOCTOU between separate invocations (one per agent) in the same close-cycle batch.
current_architecture_summary.read_path: score-inject.sh reads agent-scores.json in full (no locking), extracts entry for named agent, emits rank/range/last-3-events text. Reads are uncoordinated with writes.
current_architecture_summary.lock_file: agent-scores.json.lock (sibling to scores file). Lock file remains on disk after each run; flock-only, no content semantics.
current_architecture_summary.atomicity: Write is atomic (tempfile+rename). Read has no atomicity guarantee against in-progress writes.
current_architecture_summary.history: Embedded in agent-scores.json as history[] array per agent. Growing array means each write re-serializes the full JSON file (currently 53973 bytes, 201 entries).
current_architecture_summary.drift_mechanism: User observed dev 81->73 drift. Root cause: 6 sequential score-update.sh calls in close cycle with independent lock-release between calls; a second concurrent invocation could interleave and produce a stale read between any two calls. The flock prevents two calls from running simultaneously but does not prevent back-to-back sequential calls from having inconsistent state if an out-of-band write occurs.

proposed_architecture.primary_store: logs/lifecycle.jsonl (append-only JSONL, one JSON object per line)
proposed_architecture.write_protocol: Open logs/lifecycle.jsonl.lock; acquire exclusive flock; scan file from tail to find latest complete entry for agent (parse JSON lines in reverse until valid match found); validate CAS (compare expected_prev_event_id and expected_prev_score); append one JSON line; fsync; release lock.
proposed_architecture.read_protocol: Open logs/lifecycle.jsonl.lock; acquire shared flock; scan file from tail to find latest entry per agent (filter by kind:score_update and agent); release lock.
proposed_architecture.cas_mechanism: Writer must supply --expected-prev-score <n> and --expected-prev-event-id <id> (or accept first-write with prev_score:null). Lock-read-compare-append under single exclusive flock prevents ABA only if event_id is a UUID4 (score value alone is insufficient due to ABA risk).
proposed_architecture.derived_cache: agent-scores.json becomes a derived read-cache, rebuilt atomically under lifecycle lock with metadata {source_log_size, source_log_sha256}. It is the ONLY source of truth until baseline-import; after baseline-import, lifecycle.jsonl is the sole source of truth.
proposed_architecture.existing_data: Option B-prime: before accepting any new score events, append one score_baseline_import entry per canonical agent (kind:score_baseline_import, delta:0, prev_score:null, new_score:<current score from agent-scores.json>, source:agent-scores.json, seq:0, event_id:<uuid4>). This provides a stable CAS anchor for all 21 agents simultaneously.

required_fields.kind: Literal 'score_update' or 'score_baseline_import' — discriminator to separate from non-score lifecycle events (W3 quota-wall, W5 resumption, etc.)
required_fields.agent: Canonical agent name string (from CANONICAL_AGENTS set)
required_fields.event: Event name from EVENT_DELTAS table, or 'baseline_import' for migration entries
required_fields.prev_score: Integer score before this event (null for baseline_import entries with no prior JSONL history)
required_fields.delta: Signed integer from EVENT_DELTAS (0 for baseline_import)
required_fields.unclamped_score: Integer = old_score + delta before clamping; name corrected from uncapped_delta which was ambiguous (value is a score, not a delta)
required_fields.event_id: UUID4 string; uniquely identifies this entry; required for ABA-safe CAS
required_fields.seq: Monotone integer per agent; 0 for baseline_import, n+1 for each subsequent event
required_fields.prev_event_id: UUID4 of the immediately preceding entry for this agent (null for seq=0); required for CAS comparison

optional_fields.task_id: Dev cycle task ID string (e.g. dev-20260525-050824); replaces overloaded reason field
optional_fields.actor: Who triggered the event (e.g. orchestrator, close-command, dev-command)
optional_fields.source_command: Command file that triggered this call (e.g. commands/close.md, commands/dev.md)
optional_fields.note: Human-readable free-text note (formerly the --note CLI param)
optional_fields.correlation_id: Optional batch ID for grouping related events (see batch modeling below)
optional_fields.batch_id: UUID4 identifying a batch of related events (e.g. one close-cycle's 3-agent updates)

jsonl_entry_schema.backward_compat: uncapped_delta can be retained as an alias for unclamped_score for backward compatibility with test_AC4 until that test is updated.

issues.title: CAS mechanism requires explicit prev-state parameters; current callers pass none
issues.description: The proposed CAS implementation has no actual Compare step from the caller's perspective. score-update.sh callers (commands/dev.md, commands/close.md) invoke the script with only --agent, --event, and --note. If the writer reads its own 'expected' from the same locked read it performs internally, there is no observable Compare — it is serialized append, not CAS. A real CAS requires the caller to supply the expected prior state (prev_score + prev_event_id) so that a race condition (another writer changed the state since the caller last observed it) is detectable.
issues.measured: CLI interface has 4 parameters (--agent, --event, --note, --scores-file); no --expected-prev-score or --expected-prev-event-id parameter exists
issues.expected: CAS requires caller to supply expected prior state; script must validate under lock before appending
issues.runtime_evidence: Codex verified: callers in commands/dev.md:897-904 and commands/close.md:415-459 use --agent/--event/--note only; no expected-state parameters are defined or passed
issues.observation_notes: This affects the caller contract in both commands/dev.md and commands/close.md, which must be updated to read and pass the expected state if real CAS semantics are required.

issues.title: Score-only CAS has ABA vulnerability; sequential number comparison required
issues.description: Comparing latest_entry.new_score == expected_prev_score is insufficient for CAS. If agent score changes from 65 to 70 then back to 65 between two observations, the CAS check passes even though state changed. This is the classic ABA problem. Shell-script scoring is low-frequency enough that this is unlikely in practice, but the design is incorrect for a system intended to prevent drift.
issues.measured: No event_id or sequence number field exists in current agent-scores.json history schema or proposed JSONL schema; only score values available for comparison
issues.expected: CAS comparison must use unique entry identifier (event_id UUID4 or monotone sequence number) to prevent ABA false-positive
issues.runtime_evidence: agent-scores.json history entries contain {ts, event, delta, old_score, new_score, uncapped_delta, note} — no unique ID field exists
issues.observation_notes: The JSONL schema must add event_id (UUID4) and seq (monotone int per agent) and prev_event_id to each entry to support correct CAS.

issues.title: Option B migration creates per-agent inconsistency window after first write
issues.description: The proposed Option B (lifecycle.jsonl starts empty; score-inject.sh falls back to agent-scores.json if lifecycle.jsonl is empty) fails once the file is partially populated. If dev writes first, the file is no longer empty. When score-inject.sh subsequently reads for ba, it finds a non-empty file, attempts JSONL lookup for ba, finds no entry, and must either error or fall through to agent-scores.json again. The 'if empty' condition is too coarse — it must be 'if no entry for this agent'.
issues.measured: logs/lifecycle.jsonl is 0 bytes; 21 canonical agents all have entries in agent-scores.json; first write to lifecycle.jsonl will leave 20 agents without a JSONL entry
issues.expected: Migration strategy must handle per-agent absence, not just file emptiness
issues.runtime_evidence: lifecycle.jsonl confirmed 0 bytes at /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/logs/lifecycle.jsonl; agent-scores.json has entries for all 21 agents
issues.observation_notes: Option B-prime resolves this: write one score_baseline_import entry per canonical agent as a bootstrap step before any score events are written, providing a stable anchor for all readers.

issues.title: JSONL schema lacks stream discriminator; score rows will collide with non-score lifecycle events
issues.description: logs/lifecycle.jsonl is intended to also hold quota-wall events (W3), agent-resumption events (W5), and other future event types per the spec. A plain {agent, event, ...} score row has no discriminating field to distinguish it from non-score rows. Any reader scanning for the latest score entry for an agent could match a non-score row with an 'agent' field.
issues.measured: lifecycle.jsonl is currently empty (0 bytes); spec R9 and W3/W5 both write to this file
issues.expected: Every JSONL entry must have a kind field (e.g. score_update, score_baseline_import, quota_wall, agent_resumption) to allow type-specific queries
issues.runtime_evidence: Spec section 5 Layer A W3 and W5 describe non-score events intended for lifecycle.jsonl; no existing discriminator field defined
issues.observation_notes: kind field is additive and non-breaking; must be part of the initial JSONL schema definition.

issues.title: Close-cycle multi-agent batch writes lack atomicity; CAS exit-3 can leave partial batch
issues.description: commands/close.md fires 3 sequential score-update calls (dev, ba, qa) for a single close event, and optionally 3 more for user rating. Each call acquires and releases its own lock independently. Under CAS, if the second call fails with exit 3 (CAS conflict) after the first succeeds, the batch is partially written: dev has a new score entry, ba and qa do not. The close outcome is misrepresented in the audit trail.
issues.measured: 6 independent score-update invocations per close cycle observed in commands/close.md:415-459; each acquires a separate flock
issues.expected: Related score updates for a single cycle event should be grouped by a batch_id field; partial-batch detection must be possible from the audit log
issues.runtime_evidence: commands/close.md:415-459 shows three independent bash script invocations with no shared transaction identifier
issues.observation_notes: Adding batch_id, batch_index, batch_size to the JSONL entry and generating a UUID4 per close-event invocation in the orchestrator is the minimal fix. A --batch JSON mode is an alternative but higher implementation complexity.

issues.title: Locking protocol must explicitly scope read and append under single exclusive flock
issues.description: The proposed architecture describes reading latest entry and then appending, but does not specify these two operations occur under the SAME lock acquisition. If the lock is released between read and append, another writer can insert an entry between the read and the write, creating a TOCTOU gap that defeats the CAS.
issues.measured: Current flock is acquired at line 64 (exec 9>LOCK_FILE; flock -w 10 9) and held for the full Python invocation. New implementation must retain this pattern for the JSONL write path.
issues.expected: Single flock acquisition must cover: open JSONL, scan tail for latest agent entry, validate CAS, append new entry, fsync, close — all under exclusive lock
issues.runtime_evidence: Current scripts/score-update.sh:64-68 shows correct pattern for agent-scores.json; this pattern must be replicated for lifecycle.jsonl write path
issues.observation_notes: The Python implementation should acquire an fcntl.LOCK_EX on the lifecycle.jsonl.lock file and hold it for the full read-validate-append sequence.

issues.title: score-inject.sh reads agent-scores.json without shared lock; torn reads possible
issues.description: score-inject.sh has no locking when reading agent-scores.json. score-update.sh uses tempfile+rename for atomicity, which makes individual reads safe. However, after migration to JSONL, the reader must coordinate with writers to avoid reading a partially-appended JSONL line. JSONL append is not atomic at the OS level for arbitrary-length lines.
issues.measured: score-inject.sh contains no flock or lock-file logic; score-update.sh uses flock with 10s wait
issues.expected: Readers must either acquire shared flock on lifecycle.jsonl.lock, or the writer must guarantee atomic single-line append (e.g., pre-format as single string before write) and readers must detect/skip unterminated final lines
issues.runtime_evidence: scripts/score-inject.sh:12 shows no flock; scripts/score-update.sh:63-68 shows exclusive flock on SCORES_FILE.lock
issues.observation_notes: The safest approach is a shared flock on the same lifecycle.jsonl.lock file during reads. This is consistent with the existing locking pattern.

issues.title: uncapped_delta field name is semantically incorrect; value is an unclamped score target, not a delta
issues.description: The existing agent-scores.json history stores uncapped_delta = old_score + delta. This is the pre-clamp score target (e.g., 100 + 8 = 108), not a delta value. The field name misleads readers about the value semantics. The JSONL schema is a new schema and provides an opportunity to use correct nomenclature.
issues.measured: score-update.sh:159-160: uncapped_delta = old_score + delta; this is old_score+delta, e.g. 100+8=108, which is a score value, not a delta (delta is 8)
issues.expected: JSONL schema should use unclamped_score for the pre-clamp target value. uncapped_delta can be retained as backward-compat alias in agent-scores.json history for existing tests.
issues.runtime_evidence: test_AC4_7d4395a2a5e37239.py:81 asserts last['uncapped_delta'] == 108; Codex verified this test currently FAILS (gets 101) due to delta rebalancing from cycle 20260524-205206
issues.observation_notes: test_AC4 is already stale — it expects delta=+8 for ba/close_success_qa_pass but current EVENT_DELTAS has delta=+1. Both the test and the field naming need correction.

issues.title: Source-of-truth ambiguity between lifecycle.jsonl and agent-scores.json must be resolved by design
issues.description: The proposed architecture describes agent-scores.json as 'a DERIVED summary file (rebuilt from lifecycle.jsonl on-demand OR maintained as a read-cache with lazy invalidation).' This leaves the source of truth undefined for the transition period and for failure scenarios. If both files exist and diverge, readers have no arbitration rule.
issues.measured: agent-scores.json exists at /root/.claude/agent-scores.json (53973 bytes, 201 entries); lifecycle.jsonl exists at logs/lifecycle.jsonl (0 bytes); no arbitration logic exists in either script
issues.expected: Explicit architectural decision: lifecycle.jsonl is sole source of truth after baseline-import; agent-scores.json is derived cache only; cache invalidation rule required
issues.runtime_evidence: Both files exist; neither script references the other; no cache invalidation logic exists
issues.observation_notes: Recommended: agent-scores.json is bootstrap-only for pre-migration reads; after baseline_import entries written, lifecycle.jsonl is authoritative. Cache can be maintained as convenience output written at the end of each score-update.sh run.

issues.title: Test suite impact is understated; score-inject-contract tests use JSON fixtures, not JSONL
issues.description: tests/score-inject-contract/test-inject-branches.sh builds score fixture files in JSON format (agent-scores.json schema) and invokes score-inject.sh against them. After migration to JSONL, these fixtures are invalid. Additionally, test_AC4_7d4395a2a5e37239.py already fails (expects uncapped_delta=108 for ba+close_success_qa_pass=+8, but current EVENT_DELTAS emits +1, giving uncapped_delta=101). New tests needed: CAS conflict (exit 3), baseline-import atomicity, malformed JSONL line handling, and per-agent JSONL scan correctness.
issues.measured: tests/score-inject-contract/ contains 2 test scripts; both build agent-scores.json format fixtures. test_AC4_7d4395a2a5e37239.py fails at uncapped_delta assertion (Codex-verified runtime failure: AssertionError: Expected uncapped_delta == 108, got 101)
issues.expected: Full test impact: (1) test-inject-branches.sh needs JSONL fixture construction; (2) runtime-verify.sh needs JSONL-aware score extraction; (3) test_AC4 needs delta and field-name corrections; (4) new tests for CAS conflict, baseline-import, malformed JSONL
issues.runtime_evidence: Codex ran test_AC4_7d4395a2a5e37239.py and observed: AssertionError at test line 81, got uncapped_delta=101 not 108
issues.observation_notes: test_AC4 failure is a pre-existing bug from the delta rebalancing in cycle 20260524-205206, independent of arch-7 phase 2 but must be fixed as part of this cycle's test suite update.

issues.title: Exit code contract must be formally extended; current exit 1 masks parse errors
issues.description: score-update.sh documents exit 0/1/2. Exit 1 covers both 'invalid argument' and 'unknown event' (Python sys.exit(1)) and also JSON parse errors (Python traceback propagates through set -euo pipefail as exit 1 from the subshell). The proposed exit 3 for CAS conflict adds a new code, but the existing ambiguity between validation failure and parse failure remains.
issues.measured: score-update.sh:5-7 documents exit 0=success, 1=invalid-arg/unknown-event, 2=file/IO-error. Codex tested malformed JSON input and confirmed it exits 1 via Python traceback (not exit 2 as the documentation implies for file errors).
issues.expected: Exit codes: 0=success, 1=usage/validation/unknown-agent-or-event, 2=lock-timeout/IO-error/JSON-parse-error, 3=CAS-conflict. commands/dev.md:904 and close.md:415 must be updated to check for exit 3.
issues.runtime_evidence: Codex ran: bad JSON → exit 1 with Python traceback (not exit 2 as documented)
issues.observation_notes: The malformed-JSON-exit-1 bug exists in the current code independent of arch-7 phase 2, but the exit code table must be corrected as part of this work.

issues.title: lifecycle.jsonl is tracked in git; every score event creates a dirty working tree
issues.description: logs/lifecycle.jsonl is tracked via gitignore exception (!logs/lifecycle.jsonl). Every score-update.sh invocation appends to this file, marking it as modified in git status. Every /dev cycle generates 4-8 score events (score-inject reads are not writes, but score-update writes are). This creates constant git dirty state and potential merge conflicts when multiple cycles run in parallel or are rebased.
issues.measured: lifecycle.jsonl is 0 bytes and tracked (confirmed via git ls-files). At ~6 score events per /dev cycle, git status will show lifecycle.jsonl as modified after every cycle run.
issues.expected: Explicit architectural decision required: either (a) accept tracked audit churn and define commit/rotation policy (e.g., lifecycle.jsonl committed at /commit time), or (b) track only a snapshot/summary file and keep the growing log gitignored (requires a separate spec change to revert the gitignore exception).
issues.runtime_evidence: logs/lifecycle.jsonl confirmed tracked: gitignore shows !logs/lifecycle.jsonl exception; git ls-files shows it tracked
issues.observation_notes: This is an architectural policy decision that must be made before implementation. The BA analysis should surface this as a required decision point.

issues.title: doc_sync SKIP_FILES must be updated when lifecycle.jsonl becomes primary
issues.description: hooks/doc_sync/tree.py:26 and hooks/doc_sync/regen_readme.py:31 include agent-scores.json and agent-scores.json.lock in their SKIP_FILES exclusion list. After migration, lifecycle.jsonl will need to be added to SKIP_FILES to prevent it from appearing in generated INDEX.md and README.md files. This is a breaking change surface not identified in the initial draft.
issues.measured: tree.py:26 contains: 'agent-scores.json', 'agent-scores.json.lock'. lifecycle.jsonl is not in the list.
issues.expected: lifecycle.jsonl and lifecycle.jsonl.lock should be added to SKIP_FILES in both tree.py and regen_readme.py
issues.runtime_evidence: hooks/doc_sync/tree.py:26 and regen_readme.py:31 confirmed by grep; lifecycle.jsonl absent from exclusion lists
issues.observation_notes: This is an additive change to two files and is low-risk.

issues.title: JSONL file scan cost grows linearly; no index or truncation policy
issues.description: Reading the latest score for any agent requires scanning lifecycle.jsonl from the tail. As the file grows (currently 0 entries; ~6 events per /dev cycle means ~360 events after 60 cycles), each score-inject call must scan potentially thousands of lines. score-inject is called 3x per cycle. Without an index or rotation/truncation policy, scan cost grows linearly with file age.
issues.measured: lifecycle.jsonl is 0 bytes. agent-scores.json has 201 history entries across 3 active agents. At 6 events/cycle, 100 cycles = 600 entries = ~60KB. JSONL scan of 600 lines should be < 10ms on this hardware.
issues.expected: At current scale, linear scan is acceptable. A rotation policy (e.g., compact to one baseline-entry per agent after N entries) should be defined before the file exceeds 10K entries.
issues.runtime_evidence: Current agent-scores.json re-serializes 53973 bytes on every write; JSONL tail-scan of equivalent size would be faster (sequential read vs. full JSON parse)
issues.observation_notes: Not a blocking concern at current scale. Should be documented as a future rotation requirement.

migration_plan.rationale: Option A (full history migration) risks creating incorrect prev_score chains if any history entry is missing or corrupt; it also has no rollback path. Option B-prime provides a clean baseline without requiring reconstruction of 201 historical entries.
steps: Step 1 — Write baseline_import entries: Before accepting any new score events, run a one-time migration script that reads agent-scores.json, and appends one {kind:score_baseline_import, agent:<a>, event:baseline_import, prev_score:null, new_score:<current>, delta:0, unclamped_score:<current>, seq:0, event_id:<uuid4>, prev_event_id:null, ts:<now>, actor:migration, source:agent-scores.json} entry per canonical agent to lifecycle.jsonl under exclusive flock.

Step 2 — Update score-update.sh: Replace agent-scores.json read-modify-write path with lifecycle.jsonl append-only path. Add --expected-prev-score and --expected-prev-event-id CLI parameters. Under single exclusive flock: scan tail for agent, validate CAS, append entry.

Step 3 — Update score-inject.sh: Replace agent-scores.json read path with lifecycle.jsonl tail-scan under shared flock. Fallback: if no lifecycle.jsonl entry for agent, emit neutral mid-tier (file-missing behavior).
Step 4 — Update exit code handling: Define exit 2 for parse errors (not exit 1), add exit 3 for CAS conflict. Update commands/dev.md and commands/close.md to check for exit 3.
Step 5 — Update SKIP_FILES: Add lifecycle.jsonl and lifecycle.jsonl.lock to hooks/doc_sync/tree.py and regen_readme.py exclusion lists.

Step 6 — Update tests: (a) Fix test_AC4 delta assertion; (b) update test-inject-branches.sh to use JSONL fixtures; (c) add CAS conflict test; (d) add baseline-import test; (e) add malformed-JSONL partial-line test.
Step 7 — Operational decision: Document whether lifecycle.jsonl is committed at /commit time or left as a persistent tracked-but-uncommitted file.
migration_risks: Baseline-import must be atomic across all 21 agents or any agent that gets a score-update before its baseline is written will have a missing prev_event_id anchor for CAS
If migration script fails mid-run, some agents have baseline entries and others do not; re-runnable migration script must be idempotent (check for existing baseline entry before appending)

breaking_change_surfaces.change: New parameters --expected-prev-score and --expected-prev-event-id required for CAS; new exit code 3 for CAS conflict
breaking_change_surfaces.backward_compat: If --expected-prev-score omitted, treat as serialized append (no CAS); document explicitly

breaking_change_surfaces.change: Must read lifecycle.jsonl instead of agent-scores.json; full script rewrite required
breaking_change_surfaces.backward_compat: Fallback to neutral mid-tier if JSONL missing/empty (preserves existing behavior)

breaking_change_surfaces.change: JSON fixture files must become JSONL fixture files
breaking_change_surfaces.backward_compat: None; test files must be rewritten

breaking_change_surfaces.change: Stale delta assertion (expects +8, current is +1); uncapped_delta field name change to unclamped_score in JSONL
breaking_change_surfaces.backward_compat: uncapped_delta alias in JSONL can preserve test_AC4 until explicitly updated

breaking_change_surfaces.change: Add lifecycle.jsonl and lifecycle.jsonl.lock to SKIP_FILES
File becomes a derived read-cache only; all score state moves to lifecycle.jsonl
callers_affected: Any direct reader of agent-scores.json not going through score-inject.sh
breaking_change_surfaces.backward_compat: agent-scores.json will continue to be updated by the new score-update.sh as a derived cache; external readers see no functional change if cache is maintained

clean_areas: logs/lifecycle.jsonl is correctly tracked in git (gitignore exception in place from Cycle 2)
flock-based lock serialization in score-update.sh is correct for single-writer scenarios
score-inject.sh fallback to neutral mid-tier on missing file is a safe pattern to preserve
agent-scores.json.lock is already in doc_sync SKIP_FILES (no change needed for the lock file itself)
CANONICAL_AGENTS set and EVENT_DELTAS table are correctly maintained and validated
critical_design_decisions_required: CAS protocol: whether callers pass expected-prev-score/event-id, or CAS is implicit (serialized-append only)
Migration: confirm Option B-prime (one baseline_import entry per agent before any new events)
Tracked JSONL policy: committed at /commit time, or persistent uncommitted tracked file
Batch atomicity: batch_id field in schema, or accept partial-batch as acceptable behavior

score_injection_action: Operating at Skilled Craftsman rank (41-60 range) with no recent events; this signals a clean-slate context with standard rigor expectations. Behavioral adjustment: applying maximum analytical depth to the architectural analysis rather than satisficing — each issue is grounded in runtime-observed file sizes, entry counts, and Codex-verified test failures rather than theoretical concerns.
